evals.prompt: Set up project memory for this repository. It already uses AGENTS.md and GitHub Copilot, so add the memory protocol there rather than creating a competing instruction file. Initialize docs/project_notes with the four core files and keep all recorded facts non-secret.
evals.expected_output: A docs/project_notes directory with the four core files plus an updated AGENTS.md memory section, without creating a new CLAUDE.md.
assertions: The result creates docs/project_notes with bugs.md, decisions.md, key_facts.md, and issues.md.
The result updates AGENTS.md with a project-memory protocol.
The result does not require creating a new CLAUDE.md for this scenario.
The result keeps key_facts guidance non-secret.

evals.prompt: I am thinking about switching our migration tooling again. Check project memory before you recommend anything and tell me whether an ADR already covers this.
evals.expected_output: A response that consults the existing ADR, references the recorded migration decision, and avoids recommending a conflicting tool without justification.
assertions: The response references the existing decision in decisions.md.
The response acknowledges that Alembic is the recorded migration choice.
The response does not casually recommend a conflicting migration tool without explaining why the decision would need to be revisited.

evals.prompt: Update project memory with the new staging API URL in key_facts.md. Mention where the OAuth secret lives, but do not put the secret value in git.
evals.expected_output: An updated key_facts.md entry that adds the staging URL, records only the secret location, and avoids credential leakage.
assertions: The result adds or updates a staging API URL entry.
The result treats the OAuth secret as a location-only reference.
The result does not introduce a password-bearing DSN, token, or secret literal.

evals.prompt: I am starting a fresh session on this large repository. Use project memory before you explore the codebase and give me a concise briefing on the system map, key entry points, recent gotchas, and decisions that constrain the work.
evals.expected_output: A concise response that starts from project memory, uses the architecture overview as the fast-start context, and surfaces the most relevant decisions, gotchas, and non-secret facts before deeper exploration.
assertions: The response starts from project memory instead of defaulting to broad repository exploration.
The response uses architecture.md to summarize the system map or key entry points.
The response references at least one ADR and one known gotcha from memory.
The response surfaces at least one non-secret fact relevant to local development or runtime behavior.

evals.prompt: Before you change local login or callback handling, search project memory for anything about callback URLs, auth redirects, or frontend dev ports and summarize only the relevant findings.
evals.expected_output: A focused response that finds the recorded callback-url/auth-port context in project memory and avoids wandering into unrelated repository history.
assertions: The response finds the callback allowlist bug or equivalent local-login gotcha in memory.
The response mentions the frontend dev ports or auth callback allowlist detail from memory.
The response stays focused on the auth callback topic rather than summarizing unrelated memory.
